tool.name: Phenograph Clustering CPU [SPAC] [DMAP]
tool.description: Calculate automatic phenotypes using PhenoGraph on CPU. For datasets larger than ~500K cells, use the GPU tool instead.
tool.command: echo '{"analysis": {"type": "file", "name": "output.pickle"}}' > outputs_config.json && python '$__tool_directory__/format_values.py' 'galaxy_params.json' 'cleaned_params.json' --inject-outputs --outputs-config outputs_config.json && python -c "from spac.templates.phenograph_clustering_cpu_template import run_from_json; run_from_json('cleaned_params.json')"

param.name: Upstream_Analysis
param.label: Upstream Analysis
param.help: Link to prior processed dataset in the analysis.

param.name: Table_to_Process
param.label: Table to Process
param.help: Source table to perform the computation. The default value 'Original' will use the raw dataset.

param.name: K_Nearest_Neighbors
param.label: K-Nearest Neighbors
param.help: The number of nearest neighbors to be used in creating the features neighborhood graph.

param.name: Resolution_Parameter
param.label: Resolution Parameter
param.help: The resolution parameter (>0) adjusts clustering granularity. Higher values produce more clusters.

param.name: Output_Annotation_Name
param.label: Output Annotation Name
param.help: Annotation column name added to adata.obs. Default 'phenograph'.

param.name: Number_of_Iterations
param.label: Number of Iterations
param.help: Leiden iterations. Negative value means run until no improvement.

tool.help:
**Phenograph Clustering CPU [SPAC] [DMAP]**
CPU-based PhenoGraph clustering using the `phenograph` Python package.
**When to use CPU vs GPU**
- < ~500K cells: CPU is fine - 500K - 5M cells: GPU recommended (minutes vs hours) - > 5M cells: GPU required (CPU may not complete)
**Outputs**
- analysis: pickle file containing the AnnData object with the cluster labels in ``adata.obs[<Output_Annotation_Name>]`` and provenance in ``adata.uns['phenograph_clustering_cpu']``.
